summary: Progressive sidebar that unlocks nav items as users complete milestones, with quick-win badge variant
3-state heartbeat dot on integration cards: pulse=syncing, steady=idle, red=needs attention for glanceable connection health
Xero-style contextual cross-link: when Balance Rec difference ≠ 0, show inline prompt to jump to Transaction Rec with pre-filled date range and account
Replace generic 'Start reconciling' CTA with verb+outcome copy: 'Match transactions to your bank records' + subtitle previewing next step
Undo toast (5s auto-dismiss) after check/uncheck in Balance Reconciliation transaction list to prevent silent mis-selection frustration
Split 5+ field reconciliation modal into 3-step stepper flow (Mercury/Ramp pattern) to surface hidden complexity and kill submit-then-validate
Pre-flight 'You'll need' checklist before reconciliation form to reduce 42% in-form drop-off caused by hidden clearing account field (Stripe pattern)
Remove Beta badge from Transaction Reconciliation — zero dev cost, removes trust barrier on a financial tool (501 visitors, 17% completion, zero confusion signals)
Flip reconciliation from tool-users-find to alert-users-respond-to: surface mismatch notifications on dashboard with pre-filled reconciliation links to bypass top-of-funnel drop-off
Skeleton screens for Synder Insights: ghost outlines matching chart/KPI card shapes with shimmer pulse to reduce perceived load time and eliminate 'is it broken?' anxiety
Stripe Dashboard's optimistic atomic toggle saves — decouple each setting from global page-ready gate to kill the 29-spinner blocker and make saves instant
Outcome-first disconnect copy: what broke, how long ago, and 'nothing is lost' reassurance — addresses #1 user fear (missing data) with zero dev cost
Add keyboard focus target to empty state builder — auto-focus CTA on load, aria-expanded on FAQ accordions, accessible by default across all 13 pages
Mercury/Ramp pattern: interactive sample data in empty states instead of just illustrations — 'Preview with sample data' toggle for data-heavy pages like Daily Summary, P&L, Insights
Prioritize empty state redesigns by page traffic: Daily Summary and Business Insights get the most eyeballs post-onboarding, redesign those first instead of going alphabetically
Ship empty state copy before illustrations — decouple text from art, push AI-generated headers/descriptions/CTAs as batch PR, swap illustrations later
Integration-aware empty states: swap trust line + FAQ based on user's connected platform (ecommerce vs SaaS) — same page, different reassurance. Start with Daily Summary and P&L.
Differentiated hover state for clickable group rows: background highlight on row body, neutral drag handle column — teaches drag vs click zones without tooltip (Notion pattern)
Airtable/Notion collapsible sections for Groups page: split Manual vs Default into collapsible groups with count badges — kills flat-list pagination awkwardness and gives hierarchy
Platform-aware label swaps in per-transaction settings: surface Xero terms for Xero users, QBO terms for QBO users — terminology map from Xero diff tab as the spec
One-time dismissible banner after QBO↔Xero platform switch explaining label terminology changes — prevents returning users from thinking the UI broke
Behavioral onboarding: remove 6-option role screen, infer user type from first integration choice instead — 67% of roles funnel to same flow anyway (Linear/Slack/Notion pattern)
Add 'Fix payment method' card at top of manage-subscription page to intercept ~620 sessions/quarter that are just fixing failed payments, not cancelling — triage lane before cancellation UI
Inline tooltips for 5 jargon terms in onboarding (Reconcile, Journal Entry, COGS, Clearing Account, Sync Mode) — 1-hour fix addressing #2 universal finding from all 3 persona reviews
Remove sync mode selection from onboarding entirely — default to Summary, surface Per-Transaction as upgrade prompt after first successful sync when user has context to decide
Animate retention screens as inline expand below selected cancellation reason instead of page swap — CSS max-height transition, 30 min dev, makes retention feel conversational (Stripe/Notion pattern)
Spotify-style 'Pause subscription' option on cancellation screen — 1-3 month pause for seasonal ecommerce sellers, targets #1 cancellation reason (too expensive), cheaper than re-acquisition
Rewrite cancellation confirmation as soft landing: end date anchor, data safety reassurance, one-click reactivate link — zero backend changes, just copy (Duolingo/Notion/Spotify pattern)

'Remember this mapping' checkbox in Transaction Recon column mapping dialog — saves field→column pairs as named template, auto-applies on matching headers next upload (QBO/Xero pattern, zero ML needed)

'What might cause this?' expandable hint below Balance Recon difference amount — static decision tree (Stripe fees, timing, rounding, manual adjustments) covers 80% of cases, no AI needed (Puzzle.io/Ramp pattern)

Fix 'Refresh data' button tooltip mismatch — says 'Refresh' but tooltip says 'Create a new reconciliation...' — change to 'Start a new reconciliation with updated data' or rename button to 'New reconciliation' (Viktor finding, zero backend changes)
Flip Not Matched tab from to-do list to progress meter: show '6 matched (12% complete)' + progress bar instead of '44 unmatched' — reframes anxiety to momentum, Stripe Radar/Brex review queue pattern

5-second undo toast after Ignore action in Transaction Recon — Gmail archive pattern, batches multiple ignores, no modal friction, highest-trust improvement on the page (Viktor finding: ignore is one-click no escape)

Linear/Superhuman-style triage mode for Not Matched tab — 'Review one at a time' shows single item + Match/Ignore/Skip actions, keyboard nav, inbox-zero framing for overwhelmed new users alongside existing table view

Rewrite empty state CTA from 'Start reconciling' to 'Match your Synder data to your bank records' + subtitle 'We'll help you find any missing transactions' — verb+outcome pattern, addresses Mike Torres finding that 'reconcile' is unexplained jargon

Progressive enhancement for pricing yearly toggle — show both prices in HTML (monthly strikethrough + yearly bold), JS collapses to toggle. No JS = still see the deal. Helps screen readers, ad blockers, crawlers.

Usage-based seat pricing for Pro tier — $275 base for 3 users + $50 per additional user instead of $700 flat for unlimited. Linear/Vercel pattern, reduces sticker shock for small teams, clearer value story as they grow

Add one-line hypothesis caption under active variant button in pricing prototype switcher (e.g. 'Testing: can a wide Pro card anchor value before $275→$700 jump?') — reframes stakeholder feedback from aesthetic to hypothesis-validation, ~15 min CSS toggle via existing body[data-version]

Stripe/Linear-style dotted-underline hover tooltips on jargon-y pricing feature names ('Skipping duplicates', 'Smart Rules', 'RevRec with up to 1k subs', '1 JS modification', 'CSM', 'Premium integrations') — lets stakeholders reviewing the 5 variants judge tier differentiation without guessing what terms mean, ~45 min fix reusing the Apr 2 onboarding jargon glossary

'Xero only' toggle at top of per-transaction audit sidebar — sets body class, one CSS rule hides non-.xero-row tr's across all 11 QBO tabs so reviewers can platform-scope the audit without tab-hopping. .xero-row and .platform-divider classes already exist from ccc9d46, ~15 min (toggle + handler + 3 CSS lines). Deliberate pivot off pricing after 3 straight days of pricing-prototype ideas.

Cookieless analytics snippet (Plausible / Umami / GoatCounter) in each prototype's <head> + trackEvent on pricing variant switcher. The 4 live prototypes (pricing, recon preview, books preview, audits hub) ship to stakeholders blind — no idea which variant gets opened or how long anyone spends. 5 min per prototype, GDPR-safe, no consent banner. Turns stakeholder review from taste debate into engagement data — directly addresses the pricing review stuck since Apr 16.

What if the variant switcher itself is blocking pricing review? 6 days of silence on 5 variants may mean reviewers are paralyzed judging 'which of 5?' when real visitors only ever see one pricing page. Add ?clean=1 URL param that hides #variantSwitcher + version label, send 5 separate clean URLs so each variant is judged as a standalone marketing page, not a comparison exhibit. ~10 min (param check + one CSS rule). Pairs with yesterday's analytics snippet — separate URLs give cleaner per-variant event data.

Every paid-tier CTA in all 5 pricing variants says 'Subscribe' — except Enterprise which correctly says 'Talk to sales'. 'Subscribe' = Netflix mental model ('billed right now'), wrong verb for SaaS trial flow and especially dissonant on the $275/mo Pro card. Likely placeholder that stuck. Fix: find-and-replace across ~20 buttons in reports/pricing-prototype/index.html — Starter → 'Start free trial', Pro → 'Start 14-day trial', Enterprise unchanged. ~5 min, zero layout shift, affects all 5 variants at once. Plausibly more conversion lift than any layout being debated. Day 8 of pricing review silence — offered to bundle with the 3 stacked ~15-min wins (Xero toggle + analytics snippet + ?clean=1) and re-send a refreshed link today.

Grepped reports/pricing-prototype/index.html: zero :focus rules. Any stakeholder reviewing via keyboard (Tab-through, screen reader, or habit) literally can't see which button or card is focused — plausibly a quiet factor in Day 9 of pricing review silence. Drop-in CSS block (:focus-visible outline: 2px solid #0066ff; offset 2px) pasted into each prototype's <style> in ~5 min total across all 4 live prototypes (pricing + recon preview + books preview + audits hub). :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none } keeps mouse clicks clean. WCAG 2.4.7 formal reason; 'reviewer doesn't get lost mid-review' real reason. Fifth low-effort win now stacked — offered to bundle with yesterday's 4 (Xero toggle + analytics + ?clean=1 + Subscribe rewrite) and push as one PR.

Inline data-provenance captions on RevRec Billing v1 (built yesterday). Plaid, Modern Treasury, Brex have all moved to a 'where-did-this-number-come-from' sidekick next to every big financial figure: 'Based on 1,247 active subs • Last synced 14m ago • View calculation'. Becoming table-stakes in 2026 fintech because bookkeepers need defensible numbers, freshness can't be assumed, and it pre-empts the #1 RevRec support ticket ('is this right?'). Perfect moment to bake provenance in *before* users form expectations rather than retrofit. ~30 min for v0: one-line caption row under each top-level metric (deferred revenue, MRR, sub count) in prototypes/revrec-billing/index.html (464 lines, confirmed exists, has financial metric copy). Deliberate pivot off pricing — 5 wins stacked there, a 6th would feel obsessive. Offered to mock the caption row.

Make the pricing prototype's <title> variant-aware. Currently every variant tab shows the same 'Synder Pricing - Choose Your Plan'. With 5 variants behind one URL, reviewers can't tell tabs apart, can't bookmark a specific variant, and can't paste a deep link saying 'I prefer this one →' without re-explaining which is which. Day 11 of pricing silence might partly be: nobody can refer to a specific variant unambiguously. Fix: ~3 lines hooked into the existing data-version switch handler — document.title = `Synder Pricing — V${n} (${variantLabel})`. Pairs naturally with the Apr 22 ?clean=1 idea — each clean-URL variant would get its own unique tab title automatically. Different lane from the 5 stacked queue items: pure metadata/reviewer ergonomics, not another card design. Verified before pitching by grepping <title> across all live prototypes — pricing one is static, RevRec/recon/audits-hub already have OK titles.

GitHub PR-style 'Reviewed' checkboxes for per-transaction audit rows. GitHub's per-file 'Viewed' checkbox is the gold standard for working through long lists — tick it, the diff collapses to a thin gray bar, state persists across tabs via localStorage. Synder's per-transaction audit has 11 QBO tabs full of rows; reviewers either re-skim everything or guess where they left off. Implementation in prototypes/per-transaction-settings/index.html (verified 31 row/tab matches): tiny <input type='checkbox'> per row with data-row-id, 8 lines of JS for localStorage persistence + .reviewed class toggle, one CSS rule (tr.reviewed { opacity: 0.4 }). ~30 min total. Pivots cleanly off pricing (Day 11 silent) — audit prototype hasn't gotten attention since Apr 20 Xero-filter idea. The two pair naturally: filter narrows scope, checkboxes track progress within scope.

Click-to-copy on RevRec Billing financial figures. Pattern from Linear/Stripe/Notion: on hover, a small clipboard icon appears next to a number; click copies the value to clipboard and a tiny 'Copied ✓' tooltip fades after 1.5s. RevRec's audience is bookkeepers/accountants — copying $124,830 into a spreadsheet or journal entry is a daily action, currently triple-click + drag-select + ⌘C. ~20 lines JS + ~10 lines CSS in prototypes/revrec-billing/index.html (464 lines, verified Apr 25 — has deferred revenue / MRR / sub count copy). Pairs with the Apr 25 provenance-caption idea: provenance answers 'where did this number come from?', click-to-copy answers 'now let me use it'. Round-robins active prototypes: RevRec (Apr 25) → pricing (Apr 26) → audit (Apr 27) → RevRec today. Day 12 of pricing silence — 3rd consecutive pivot off pricing. Sent: Telegram direct, message 543 (delivered ~30min late after MCP reconnect).

All | Pending | Approved tab filter for per-transaction audit. Default to 'Pending' so you see only what's left to review. Frontend-only — hide/show rows based on Approved column state. ~30 min to add, saves scrolling through 70 rows to find the 12 still needing decisions. Pattern from GitHub PR reviews: default to 'Files changed' (pending), with tabs for 'All' and 'Approved'.

Reframe reports index from file list to kanban dashboard: Active / Shipped / Archive columns with cards showing project name, last updated, stats, status badge. Turns hub into project management tool, makes stuck projects visible (e.g., pricing prototype 2 weeks no movement).
